demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I had a mild stomach bug yesterday, but today my muscles are completely paralyzed, and I can't catch my breath at all. (Spouse on the call adds: he's breathing really deep and fast and seems confused.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A mild stomach bug started yesterday; today he suddenly can't move his arms or legs, is breathing very deeply and fast, and seems confused and not himself.

symptom_narrative.onset: the stomach upset started about 24 hours ago; the severe muscle weakness, deep fast breathing and confusion came on today and have worsened over the last few hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about a day of stomach upset; a few hours of the severe weakness and rapid breathing
symptom_narrative.character: extreme, generalized muscle cramping and weakness — he cannot get out of bed or lift his arms or legs; breathing has turned deep and very fast
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has ever felt — completely unable to move or catch his breath
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body muscles, and his breathing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse over the last few hours
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped; he tried to drink water but keeps failing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has kept getting worse on its own since the stomach bug stopped him eating and drinking
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: vomited twice yesterday, has eaten and drunk almost nothing since; spouse says he is confused, drowsy and not himself; empty water bottles by the bed from failed attempts to rehydrate
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had milder flare-ups of weakness with past illnesses, but never anywhere near this severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally walks with mobility aids; right now he is completely bedbound and cannot stand or lift his limbs

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a mitochondrial muscle disease (mitochondrial myopathy), diagnosed years ago after a muscle biopsy
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a daily 'mito cocktail' of vitamins, paces his activity, uses mobility aids, and carries a 'mitochondrial emergency letter'

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: hearing loss in both ears since he was younger
droopy eyelids and difficulty moving his eyes
a thickened heart-muscle condition he was told about (he thinks it is called hypertrophic)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with his spouse, who is present and made the call; normally ambulant with mobility aids and independent with support

objective_data_offered.value: deep, sighing, very fast breathing that started about 3 hours ago
seems confused, drowsy and not himself, drifting in and out
pale and sweaty, very floppy and weak, cannot lift his arms or legs
4.0 mmol/L on his home glucose meter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home glucometer

objective_data_offered.value: 97% on a home fingertip pulse oximeter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home-device reading, may be affected by cool/sweaty fingers
